title: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted

seo.title: Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi | Authentic Middle Eastern Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi with our authentic Middle Eastern recipe. Tender lamb, aromatic rice, and spices for a show-stopping meal.

Step-by-Step Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
Tips for Perfect Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi
seo.imageAlt: A large platter of Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi, garnished with nuts, herbs, and served with aromatic rice.
seo.imageCaption: Impress your guests with this magnificent Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi, a true centerpiece for any Middle Eastern feast.

description: Lamb Ouzi Whole Slow Roasted is a majestic Middle Eastern dish featuring succulent, slow-cooked lamb served atop fragrant spiced rice. This impressive centerpiece is traditionally garnished with toasted nuts and fresh herbs, making it perfect for celebrations and family gatherings. The meat falls off the bone, infused with rich aromatic flavors, creating an unforgettable culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: if making own blend, plus more for seasoning

instructions.title: Prepare and Marinate the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb leg dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, combine 3 tablespoons of olive oil, the Ouzi spice blend (or your homemade mix of allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, and black pepper), and 1 tablespoon of salt. Mix well to form a thick paste. Rub this spice paste generously over the entire lamb leg, ensuring it covers all surfaces and gets into any scored areas. For maximum flavor penetration, cover the lamb and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight.

instructions.title: Slow Roast the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 300F (150C). Place the marinated lamb leg into a large, sturdy roasting pan. Pour 1 cup of lamb or chicken broth into the bottom of the pan. Cover the roasting pan tightly with heavy-duty aluminum foil to create a sealed environment for slow cooking. Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and roast for 3.5 to 4.5 hours. The exact time will depend on the size of your lamb leg and desired tenderness; it should be fork-tender and easily pull away from the bone. You can remove the foil for the last 30 minutes if you prefer a slightly browned exterior.

instructions.title: Prepare the Ouzi Rice Base
instructions.text: While the lamb is roasting, begin preparing the aromatic Ouzi rice. In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil or ghee over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onions and sauté them gently until they become soft, translucent, and slightly golden, which typically takes about 8-10 minutes. Stir frequently to prevent burning. Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute until its fragrant aroma is released.

instructions.title: Cook the Spiced Ouzi Rice
instructions.text: Add the soaked and well-drained Basmati rice to the pot with the onions and garlic. Stir the rice thoroughly to ensure each grain is coated with the aromatic mixture and a little oil. Pour in the remaining 3 cups of lamb or chicken broth, then add the frozen green peas and chopped carrots. Bring the liquid to a rolling boil over medium-high heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to its lowest setting, cover the pot tightly with a lid, and let it simmer undisturbed for 15-20 minutes, or until all the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender and fluffy.

instructions.title: Assemble the Lamb Ouzi
instructions.text: Carefully remove the slow-roasted lamb leg from the oven and transfer it to a large, impressive serving platter. Fluff the cooked Ouzi rice with a fork to separate the grains and release any steam. Spread the fragrant rice generously around the base of the serving platter. You can either place the whole lamb leg majestically on top of the rice as a centerpiece, or for easier serving, you may gently shred some of the tender lamb meat and mix it into the rice.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: To complete your magnificent Lamb Ouzi, generously sprinkle the toasted slivered almonds and pine nuts over both the lamb and the rice. These provide a delightful crunch and nutty flavor. Finally, garnish with a shower of fresh chopped parsley or cilantro for a burst of color and herbaceous freshness. Serve the Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi immediately with lemon wedges on the side, allowing guests to squeeze fresh lemon juice over their portions. It pairs wonderfully with a side of plain yogurt or a crisp Middle Eastern salad.

tips: For the most tender lamb, marinate it overnight. This allows the spices to deeply penetrate the meat and tenderize it further.
If you cannot find a pre-made Ouzi spice blend, a homemade mix of allspice, cinnamon, cardamom, nutmeg, and black pepper works beautifully.
Leftover Ouzi can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. Reheat gently, adding a splash of broth if the rice seems dry.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of the Middle East with a dish that embodies celebration, generosity, and exquisite flavor: Lamb Ouzi. More than just a meal, Ouzi is a grand centerpiece, a feast for the eyes and the palate, often served at weddings, Eid al-Adha, and other significant family gatherings. While there are many ways to prepare lamb, the most impressive and arguably the most delicious is the **Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi**. This method transforms a humble lamb into an incredibly tender, aromatic, and unforgettable experience, making it a truly iconic Middle Eastern dinner recipe. If you're looking to create a show-stopping dish that will leave a lasting impression, mastering the art of Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi is an absolute must.

## What is Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi?

Lamb Ouzi, also spelled Oozy or Ghuzi, is a renowned Middle Eastern dish featuring succulent lamb, often served over a bed of fragrant, spiced rice, typically studded with nuts and sometimes peas or minced meat. What elevates it to a truly spectacular level is the "whole slow roasted" preparation. Instead of using cuts like shoulders or legs, a young, whole lamb (or a significant portion like a whole suckling lamb) is slow-roasted for many hours until the meat is fall-off-the-bone tender and infused with a rich array of spices.

This method ensures deep flavor penetration and an incredibly moist texture, far surpassing what can be achieved with faster cooking. The slow roasting process allows the connective tissues to break down gradually, resulting in meat that practically melts in your mouth. Culturally, it's a dish of hospitality and abundance, a testament to a host's generosity, symbolizing warmth and welcome at the grandest of tables. The sheer scale and beauty of a Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi make it an undeniable showstopper, perfect for feeding a large gathering.

## Ingredients for Authentic Lamb Ouzi

Crafting the perfect Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi requires careful selection of ingredients. Here's what you'll need:

For the Whole Lamb:
*   1 whole young lamb (typically 20-30 lbs for a large gathering, or a large leg/shoulder if scaling down for a smaller crowd, though the article focuses on whole)
*   1/2 cup olive oil
*   1 whole head garlic, cloves peeled and minced
*   1/4 cup fresh lemon juice
*   1/4 cup salt
*   2 tablespoons black pepper
*   2 tablespoons ground cumin
*   2 tablespoons ground coriander
*   1 tablespoon sweet paprika
*   1 tablespoon ground allspice
*   1 teaspoon ground cardamom
*   1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
*   1 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon ground cloves (optional)

For the Spiced Rice Filling (to be served alongside or lightly stuffed):
*   4 cups basmati rice, rinsed and soaked for 30 minutes
*   1/4 cup olive oil
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 lb ground lamb (optional, but traditional for some versions)
*   1 cup frozen peas
*   1/2 cup pine nuts, toasted
*   1/2 cup slivered almonds, toasted
*   2 tablespoons Ouzi spice blend (or a mix of cumin, coriander, allspice, cinnamon, cardamom)
*   Salt and pepper to taste
*   6 cups hot lamb or chicken broth

For Garnish:
*   Fresh parsley or mint, chopped
*   Extra toasted pine nuts and almonds

## Step-by-Step Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Preparing a Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi is a labor of love, but the steps are straightforward and the reward is immense.

1.  **Prepare the Lamb**: Thoroughly clean the whole lamb, removing any excess fat or glands. Pat it completely dry with paper towels. Using a sharp knife, score the surface of the lamb all over, making shallow cuts about 1-inch apart. This helps the marinade penetrate deeply.

2.  **Make the Marinade**: In a bowl, combine the olive oil, minced garlic, lemon juice, salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, paprika, allspice, cardamom, cinnamon, turmeric, and optional cloves. Mix well to form a thick paste.

3.  **Marinate the Lamb**: Generously rub the entire lamb, inside and out, with the spice marinade. Make sure to get into all the scored cuts. For best results, place the lamb in a very large roasting bag or wrap it tightly in plastic wrap, then refrigerate for at least 12-24 hours. The longer it marinates, the deeper the flavor.

4.  **Pre-roast (Optional, but Recommended for Color)**: If your oven allows, preheat it to 400°F (200°C). Place the lamb in a very large, sturdy roasting pan. Roast for 30-45 minutes until the exterior is nicely browned. This step gives the lamb a beautiful crust.

5.  **Slow Roast**: Reduce the oven temperature to 275°F (135°C). Cover the roasting pan tightly with heavy-duty aluminum foil. The goal is to create a steamy environment. Slow roast for 6-8 hours, or even longer depending on the size of the lamb, until the meat is incredibly tender and easily pulls away from the bone. An internal temperature of 190-200°F (88-93°C) indicates optimal tenderness for pulled lamb. Baste the lamb every 1-2 hours with its own juices.

6.  **Rest the Lamb**: Once done, carefully remove the lamb from the oven. Let it rest, still covered, for at least 30-60 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum moisture and flavor.

7.  **Prepare the Spiced Rice**: While the lamb is resting (or during the last hour of roasting), prepare the rice. In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Sauté the chopped onion until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. If using ground lamb, add it and cook until browned, breaking it up with a spoon. Drain any excess fat. Add the soaked and drained basmati rice, peas, Ouzi spice blend, salt, and pepper. Stir well to coat the rice. Pour in the hot broth, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender. Stir in the toasted pine nuts and almonds.

8.  **Assemble and Serve**: Carefully transfer the majestic Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi to an enormous serving platter. Surround it with the fragrant spiced rice. Garnish generously with fresh parsley/mint and additional toasted nuts. Carve the lamb at the table, allowing guests to witness its incredible tenderness.

## Tips for Perfect Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi

Achieving perfection with your Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi is within reach with these expert tips:

*   **Quality Lamb is Key**: Source a young, good-quality lamb from a reputable butcher. Younger lamb tends to be more tender and has a milder flavor.
*   **Don't Skimp on Marination Time**: The long marination period is crucial for infusing the lamb with deep flavor. Plan ahead!
*   **Low and Slow is the Way to Go**: Resist the temptation to rush the roasting process. The low temperature and extended cooking time are essential for breaking down collagen and achieving that melt-in-your-mouth tenderness.
*   **Monitor Temperature**: While time is a good guide, an instant-read meat thermometer is your best friend. For truly tender, falling-apart lamb, aim for an internal temperature of 190-200°F (88-93°C).
*   **Resting is Non-Negotiable**: Just like any large roast, resting the lamb allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, resulting in a more succulent and flavorful meat.
*   **Use a Roasting Rack**: If possible, roast the lamb on a rack within your roasting pan. This allows for even cooking and prevents the bottom from getting soggy.
*   **Baste Frequently**: Regular basting with the accumulated pan juices keeps the lamb moist and enhances its flavor.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

The Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi is typically the star of the show, but it shines even brighter with the right accompaniments.

*   **Traditional Presentation**: Serve the carved lamb directly over a large bed of the spiced Ouzi rice on an impressive communal platter.
*   **Cooling Counterparts**: A refreshing yogurt sauce (like plain yogurt mixed with minced garlic, dried mint, and a pinch of salt) or a light, crisp green salad (such as Fattoush) provides a perfect contrast to the rich lamb.
*   **Pickles and Bread**: A selection of Middle Eastern pickles (torshi) and fresh pita bread or lavash are excellent for scooping up every last bit of flavor.
*   **Beverages**: For a truly authentic experience, offer Arak (an anise-flavored alcoholic drink, for adults) or simply chilled water and traditional mint tea to cleanse the palate.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi is the ultimate presentation, there are ways to adapt the recipe:

*   **Lamb Cut**: If a whole lamb is too daunting or unavailable, you can use a large leg of lamb, a shoulder, or even a bone-in lamb shank. Adjust roasting times accordingly (a leg might take 4-6 hours, a shoulder 3-5 hours). The essence of the dish remains, though the "whole" impact is diminished.
*   **Rice Filling**: Experiment with different nuts like pistachios or cashews. Dried fruits such as apricots or raisins can add a touch of sweetness. Some recipes include vermicelli noodles toasted with the rice.
*   **Spice Blend**: While a classic Ouzi spice blend is provided, you can adjust ratios or add other warm spices like ginger, baharat, or even a touch of saffron to the rice.
*   **Cooking Method**: For those with outdoor cooking capabilities, a large smoker or even a traditional underground pit (Hanak) can impart unique smoky flavors to the lamb.
*   **Vegetarian Ouzi (Rice Component)**: While not Lamb Ouzi, the spiced rice itself is so flavorful that it can be adapted into a vegetarian main course by adding roasted vegetables like eggplant, zucchini, and bell peppers, topped with nuts.

The **Whole Slow Roasted Lamb Ouzi** is more than just a recipe; it's a testament to the rich culinary heritage of the Middle East, a dish that brings people together and creates lasting memories. While it requires time and effort, the result is an incredibly tender, aromatic, and deeply flavorful lamb that will undoubtedly be the highlight of any special occasion. Gather your loved ones, prepare this magnificent feast, and savor the true taste of Middle Eastern hospitality.
